codeReview.summary: Re-review (round 2) confirms the shared-ui-components feature remains fully valid. All 4 UI primitives (AnimatedModal, Toast, Skeleton, Button) are correctly implemented under components/ui/ with proper motion animations, TypeScript interfaces, and test coverage. AnimatedModal uses m.div with AnimatePresence, isOpen/onClose props, and closes on backdrop click. Toast system provides ToastProvider context + useToast hook with success/error/info types, 3s auto-dismiss, and slide-from-top animation; it's mounted globally in LayoutWrapper. Skeleton components include base Skeleton with shimmer CSS animation, ProductCardSkeleton, and MatchCardSkeleton variants. Button uses m.button with whileTap scale(0.97) and supports primary/secondary/outline/ghost variants. All loading spinners replaced with skeletons across 6 pages. Zero animate-spin patterns remain in app/ or components/. All 278 tests pass (including 29 new component tests), typecheck clean.

issues.description: Toast ID uses Date.now() which could theoretically produce duplicate IDs if showToast is called twice in the same millisecond tick. A counter or crypto.randomUUID() would be safer, though in practice this is unlikely to cause issues with user-triggered toasts.

Skeleton uses both Tailwind animate-pulse class AND inline style animation with 'pulse' and 'shimmer'. The inline animation overrides the Tailwind class, making the class redundant. Minor styling inconsistency but functionally correct.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md correctly documents that shared UI components (Button, AnimatedModal, Toast, Skeleton) should be used. The worker followed all conventions: 'use client' directives, m components from motion/react, semantic Tailwind tokens, proper TypeScript interfaces.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All 4 component files use 'use client', import from 'motion/react', use m.div/m.button, and use semantic tokens like bg-primary-500, shadow-elevated, rounded-card.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The 'useToast must be used within ToastProvider' error message pattern is a good practice documented implicitly by implementation but not in AGENTS.md. Other context hooks in the project should follow this pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: components/ui/Toast.tsx:24 — throws clear error if useToast used outside provider

summary: Round 2 re-review: shared-ui-components feature passes. All 4 components (AnimatedModal, Toast, Skeleton, Button) are correctly implemented with motion animations, proper TypeScript types, and comprehensive tests. All spinners replaced across 6 pages (0 animate-spin matches). 278 tests pass, typecheck clean. Two non-blocking style observations noted (Date.now() for toast IDs, redundant Tailwind class on Skeleton). No blocking issues.
